analysis_result: Only 2 of 4 MPNST cell lines (S462, S462TY) showed significantly elevated GLI1 expression relative to HSC1λ at alpha=0.05. S462: 12.79-fold (p=0.0219); S462TY: 5.21-fold (p=0.0461); T265: 1.09-fold (p=0.8979, NS); STS-26T: 0.24-fold (significantly REDUCED). Paper reports 3 of 4 (S462, S462TY, T265) as elevated.
discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED: MOESM10 Fig 5j sheet containing qPCR fold-change values for GLI1 across 4 MPNST cell lines (S462, S462TY, T265, STS-26T) relative to HSC1λ control, n=3 replicates per cell line.

B. COHORT: 4 MPNST cell lines vs HSC1λ control reference; n=3 technical/biological replicates each.

C. VARIABLE CODING: GLI1 fold-change expression values from Fig 5j qPCR data; t-test comparison at alpha=0.05.

D. COMPUTATION: Two-sample t-test of each MPNST line vs HSC1λ reference. Results: S462 mean=12.79, p=0.0219 (significant elevation); S462TY mean=5.21, p=0.0461 (significant elevation); T265 mean=1.09, p=0.8979 (NOT significant despite slight mean elevation); STS-26T mean=0.24, significantly REDUCED below control.

E. RESULT: 2 of 4 MPNST lines significantly elevated (S462, S462TY). T265 shows slight mean elevation but high variance yields non-significance. STS-26T is significantly reduced.

F. COMPARISON WITH PAPER: Paper reports 3 of 4 elevated (S462, S462TY, T265). Model finds only 2 of 4 meet significance threshold. Qualitative direction (mean elevated) agrees for T265, but statistical significance differs.

G. ROOT CAUSE: Likely differences in statistical methodology. The paper may have used: (1) a one-sample t-test against fold-change=1 rather than two-sample test vs control replicates, (2) no multiple-testing correction and a less stringent significance threshold, (3) a directional (one-tailed) test, or (4) pooled variance estimates across genes. T265's high variance on n=3 replicates drives non-significance in the model's two-sample formulation. The qualitative finding (3 lines show elevated mean GLI1) is preserved; only the significance call on T265 differs.
proposed_fix: Reproduce the paper's result by using a one-sample t-test against a null hypothesis of fold-change=1 (rather than a two-sample test against HSC1λ replicates), or use a one-tailed test for elevation, or relax the alpha threshold. Alternatively, confirm whether paper pooled variance across multiple genes (GLI1, GLI2, SMO) to increase power for detecting T265 elevation.
